metadata.title: FLAb: Benchmarking deep learning methods for antibody fitness prediction

figures.description: NeurIPS and Johns Hopkins University logos

sections.title: 1 Background
content: Antibody therapeuticsmust have a particular set offitness properties
Six classes of biophysical data relevant to antibody development are (a) expression, (b) thermostability, (c) immunogenicity, (d) binding affinity, (e) aggregation, and (f) polyreactivity
Dodeep learning methodscapture properties of therapeutic antibodies?
1. Creating a collection of datasets of antibody therapeutic data
2. Benchmark representative AI model likelihoods for fitness prediction

sections.title: 2 Dataset curation
content: We curated publicly availableantibody fitness datasets

This database includes mutational landscapes of 17 distinct antibody families and a total of 13,384 associated fitness metrics. Each sequence is mapped to at least one fitness label pertaining to the 6 fitness properties.

tables.description: Summary table of antibody fitness datasets and number of datapoints

sections.title: 3 Methods
content: For each protein language model, we separately input the antibodyheavyandlight sequencesto return twoperplexity scores, and we tabulate the average perplexity between the two sequences. We calculate Spearman's (ρ), Pearson's (r) and Kendall tau's (τ) correlation coefficients betweenperplexityandfitness.

figures.description: Workflow diagram showing antibody sequence input to language model and fitness metric correlation

sections.title: 4 Results
content: Summary of Pearson's correlations. Models generally perform best with thermostability and binding affinity datasets, but most struggle with aggregation propensity and polyreactivity.
Intrinsicbiophysical properties are more accurately predicted thanextrinsic
Models are more accurate at distinguishingintra-familyversusinter-familyantibody sets
Parameter sizeinfluences performance over architecture and dataset composition
Some models favorevolutionary signalrather thanphysical fitness

figures.description: Heatmap showing Pearson correlations across various models and datasets
Boxplots comparing Pearson correlations for Aggregation (Extrinsic) and Thermostability (Intrinsic)
Boxplots comparing model performance on intra-family vs inter-family thermostability datasets
Boxplot of Thermostability correlation vs ProGen2 model size
Boxplot of Polyreactivity correlation vs ProGen2 model size
Scatter plot of Perplexity vs Temperature for ProGen2-XL
Scatter plot of Rosetta Total Energy vs Temperature

sections.title: 5 Conclusion
content: FLAb is a living benchmark where we will continuously add models evaluated and available antibody data
No model was top performing across all six fitness classes
